column1: C[Uberdude: Should be b5, then after white c4 you can play here to capture b6.
C[Uberdude: White b8 instead would capture you.
C[Uberdude: Black is lucky to get away with it.
C[Uberdude: g8 better to stop white going into that corner, as even if white capture at e9 he only has 1 eye so is dead.

C[Uberdude: You don't need to play here as white e9 then black g8 and white is dead. But more complex is white g8, then black e9 and white tries to live in the corner. Pushing through the gap at e4 instead is better.
C[Uberdude: Not necessary, -1 point. You can try c4 but ti doesn't work if white answers correctly. Or just j5 endgame.
C[Uberdude: Big mistake, white should b4.
C[Uberdude: a5 is better as then white a3 is not sente, though here as there is nowhere else to play it doesn't actually make a difference.
C[illluck: Up to here I think black played ok, but feels like black should C8 instead here because the C8 cut potential helps white settle.
C[illluck: D8 is probably a bit more important here as after white D8 the black stone at E7 is damaged.
illluck: This means that if white plays move against D4 black will have trouble deciding which one to save (D8 or D4).
C[illluck: After corner lives, D8 is not so important. Consider something like F5 here instead.
C[illluck: F7 is better locally, but black probably should play something like G5 here instead - white will win if the bottom becomes white's.

illluck: Another possibility is F6, seeking to use the fact that white's four stones at D6 do not have a lot of liberties. However, it looks like white is fine (proof left as an exercise for the reader :p), so probably G5 is right.
C[illluck: Locally G7 is better, but as black I'd try something like G3.
